_doc: Each entry registers ONE deterministic computation: a metric formula,
a Postgres RPC, a Python pure function. The contract makes the math
auditable: any downstream dashboard or AI agent can cite the formula_id
and the standards reference (ISO 14224, SAE JA1011, etc.), and
regression tests can pin the math.
name            human-readable name ('MTBF — Mean Time Between Failures')
inputs          list of capture_ids / column refs the formula reads
implemented_in  RPC name OR Python function OR migration filename
Auditor: tools/audit_formula_contracts.py — surfaces RPCs that look
like deterministic metrics but aren't yet registered.
Seed entries below are the formulas already documented in KPI_ENGINE.md.

formulas.name: MTBF — Mean Time Between Failures
formulas.description: Mean of inter-arrival intervals of corrective entries within a window. Same algorithm shared by the interactive RPC (user-selected window) and the materialised view (snapshot at 30/90/365 days).

formulas.name: MTTR — Mean Time To Repair
formulas.description: Mean downtime_hours of corrective entries whose status = Closed within the window.

formulas.name: PM Compliance — 30-day floor
formulas.description: is_due = (last_anchor_date IS NULL) OR (last_anchor_date < now - 30 days). Used by every PM-overdue tile across the platform; period-scoped variants for 30/90/365 day windows are derived from the same anchor.

formulas.name: Risk Score (composite, 6-factor)
formulas.implemented_in: edge fn: batch-risk-scoring (writes asset_risk_scores) + view: v_risk_truth
formulas.description: Weighted composite — PM overdue 0.30, fault frequency 0.30, time-to-MTBF 0.20, repeat fault 0.20, Weibull wear-out 0.15, FMEA top RPN 0.10 — clamped to [0, 1]. Daily snapshot.

formulas.name: OEE — Overall Equipment Effectiveness (full)
formulas.implemented_in: RPC: get_oee_by_machine (migration 20260520000002) — falls back to partial when performance_pct is NULL
formulas.description: Full OEE = Availability × Performance × Quality. The canonical Nakajima formula. Requires three independent factors; if any factor is null/unavailable the RPC returns the partial form (see oee_iso_22400_partial) and tags the result accordingly.

formulas.name: OEE — partial (Availability × Quality)
formulas.description: Partial OEE = Availability × Quality. Used when per-asset ideal cycle time (the Performance factor input) is not yet captured. Always declared 'partial' in the source chip so users don't conflate with full OEE. Closing this gap requires capture contract asset_ideal_cycle_time (Tier F next-build).
formulas.added_in: formal contract 2026-05-20 (captures existing platform reality + flags the gap)
